[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “The devil, that’s the last straw,” he muttered, grinding his teeth, “no,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=524 b=0 i=0 f=f3] that would not suit me… just now. She is a fool,” he added aloud. “I’ll go
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=542 b=0 i=0 f=f3] and talk to her to-day.”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Fool she is and no mistake, just as I am. But why, if you are so clever,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=578 b=0 i=0 f=f3] do you lie here like a sack and have nothing to show for it? One time you
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=596 b=0 i=0 f=f3] used to go out, you say, to teach children. But why is it you do nothing
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=614 b=0 i=0 f=f3] now?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “I am doing…” Raskolnikov began sullenly and reluctantly.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “What are you doing?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Work…”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “What sort of work?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “I am thinking,” he answered seriously after a pause.

[PAGE 38]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Nastasya was overcome with a fit of laughter. She was given to laughter
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=92 b=0 i=0 f=f3] and when anything amused her, she laughed inaudibly, quivering and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=110 b=0 i=0 f=f3] shaking all over till she felt ill.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “And have you made much money by your thinking?” she managed to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=146 b=0 i=0 f=f3] articulate at last.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “One can’t go out to give lessons without boots. And I’m sick of it.”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Don’t quarrel with your bread and butter.”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “They pay so little for lessons. What’s the use of a few coppers?” he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=218 b=0 i=0 f=f3] answered, reluctantly, as though replying to his own thought.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “And you want to get a fortune all at once?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=0 f=f3] He looked at her strangely.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Yes, I want a fortune,” he answered firmly, after a brief pause.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Don’t be in such a hurry, you quite frighten me! Shall I get you the loaf
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=308 b=0 i=0 f=f3] or not?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “As you please.”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Ah, I forgot! A letter came for you yesterday when you were out.”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “A letter? for me! from whom?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “I can’t say. I gave three copecks of my own to the postman for it. Will
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=398 b=0 i=0 f=f3] you pay me back?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Then bring it to me, for God’s sake, bring it,” cried Raskolnikov
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=434 b=0 i=0 f=f3] greatly excited—“good God!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=0 f=f3] A minute later the letter was brought him. That was it: from his mother,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=470 b=0 i=0 f=f3] from the province of R—. He turned pale when he took it. It was a long
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=488 b=0 i=0 f=f3] while since he had received a letter, but another feeling also suddenly
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=506 b=0 i=0 f=f3] stabbed his heart.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Nastasya, leave me alone, for goodness’ sake; here are your three
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=542 b=0 i=0 f=f3] copecks, but for goodness’ sake, make haste and go!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=0 f=f3] The letter was quivering in his hand; he did not want to open it in her
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=578 b=0 i=0 f=f3] presence; he wanted to be left alone with this letter. When Nastasya had
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=596 b=0 i=0 f=f3] gone out, he lifted it quickly to his lips and kissed it; then he gazed intently
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=614 b=0 i=0 f=f3] at the address, the small, sloping handwriting, so dear and familiar, of the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=632 b=0 i=0 f=f3] mother who had once taught him to read and write. He delayed; he seemed
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=650 b=0 i=0 f=f3] almost afraid of something. At last he opened it; it was a thick heavy letter,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=668 b=0 i=0 f=f3] weighing over two ounces, two large sheets of note paper were covered
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=686 b=0 i=0 f=f3] with very small handwriting.

[PAGE 39]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] “My dear Rodya,” wrote his mother—“it’s two months since I last
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] had a talk with you by letter which has distressed me and even kept
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] me awake at night, thinking. But I am sure you will not blame me for
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] my inevitable silence. You know how I love you; you are all we have
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to look to, Dounia and I, you are our all, our one hope, our one stay.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] What a grief it was to me when I heard that you had given up the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] university some months ago, for want of means to keep yourself and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] that you had lost your lessons and your other work! How could I
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] help you out of my hundred and twenty roubles a year pension? The
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] fifteen roubles I sent you four months ago I borrowed, as you know,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] on security of my pension, from Vassily Ivanovitch Vahrushin a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] merchant of this town. He is a kind-hearted man and was a friend of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] your father’s too. But having given him the right to receive the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] pension, I had to wait till the debt was paid off and that is only just
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] done, so that I’ve been unable to send you anything all this time. But
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] now, thank God, I believe I shall be able to send you something
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] more and in fact we may congratulate ourselves on our good fortune
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] now, of which I hasten to inform you. In the first place, would you
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] have guessed, dear Rodya, that your sister has been living with me
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] for the last six weeks and we shall not be separated in the future.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Thank God, her sufferings are over, but I will tell you everything in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] order, so that you may know just how everything has happened and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] all that we have hitherto concealed from you. When you wrote to me
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] two months ago that you had heard that Dounia had a great deal to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] put up with in the Svidrigraïlovs’ house, when you wrote that and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] asked me to tell you all about it—what could I write in answer to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] you? If I had written the whole truth to you, I dare say you would
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] have thrown up everything and have come to us, even if you had to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] walk all the way, for I know your character and your feelings, and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] you would not let your sister be insulted. I was in despair myself, but
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] what could I do? And, besides, I did not know the whole truth myself
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] then. What made it all so difficult was that Dounia received a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] hundred roubles in advance when she took the place as governess in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] their family, on condition of part of her salary being deducted every
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] month, and so it was impossible to throw up the situation without
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] repaying the debt. This sum (now I can explain it all to you, my

[PAGE 40]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] precious Rodya) she took chiefly in order to send you sixty roubles,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] which you needed so terribly then and which you received from us
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] last year. We deceived you then, writing that this money came from
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia’s savings, but that was not so, and now I tell you all about it,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] because, thank God, things have suddenly changed for the better,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and that you may know how Dounia loves you and what a heart she
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] has. At first indeed Mr. Svidrigaïlov treated her very rudely and used
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to make disrespectful and jeering remarks at table…. But I don’t
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] want to go into all those painful details, so as not to worry you for
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] nothing when it is now all over. In short, in spite of the kind and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] generous behaviour of Marfa Petrovna, Mr. Svidrigaïlov’s wife, and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] all the rest of the household, Dounia had a very hard time,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] especially when Mr. Svidrigaïlov, relapsing into his old regimental
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] habits, was under the influence of Bacchus. And how do you think it
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was all explained later on? Would you believe that the crazy fellow
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] had conceived a passion for Dounia from the beginning, but had
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] concealed it under a show of rudeness and contempt. Possibly he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was ashamed and horrified himself at his own flighty hopes,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] considering his years and his being the father of a family; and that
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] made him angry with Dounia. And possibly, too, he hoped by his
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] rude and sneering behaviour to hide the truth from others. But at
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] last he lost all control and had the face to make Dounia an open and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] shameful proposal, promising her all sorts of inducements and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] offering, besides, to throw up everything and take her to another
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] estate of his, or even abroad. You can imagine all she went through!
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] To leave her situation at once was impossible not only on account of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] the money debt, but also to spare the feelings of Marfa Petrovna,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] whose suspicions would have been aroused: and then Dounia would
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] have been the cause of a rupture in the family. And it would have
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] meant a terrible scandal for Dounia too; that would have been
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] inevitable. There were various other reasons owing to which Dounia
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] could not hope to escape from that awful house for another six
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] weeks. You know Dounia, of course; you know how clever she is and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] what a strong will she has. Dounia can endure a great deal and even
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] in the most difficult cases she has the fortitude to maintain her
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] firmness. She did not even write to me about everything for fear of

[PAGE 41]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] upsetting me, although we were constantly in communication. It all
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] ended very unexpectedly. Marfa Petrovna accidentally overheard
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] her husband imploring Dounia in the garden, and, putting quite a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] wrong interpretation on the position, threw the blame upon her,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] believing her to be the cause of it all. An awful scene took place
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] between them on the spot in the garden; Marfa Petrovna went so far
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] as to strike Dounia, refused to hear anything and was shouting at
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] her for a whole hour and then gave orders that Dounia should be
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] packed off at once to me in a plain peasant’s cart, into which they
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] flung all her things, her linen and her clothes, all pell-mell, without
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] folding it up and packing it. And a heavy shower of rain came on,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] too, and Dounia, insulted and put to shame, had to drive with a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] peasant in an open cart all the seventeen versts into town. Only
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] think now what answer could I have sent to the letter I received from
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] you two months ago and what could I have written? I was in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] despair; I dared not write to you the truth because you would have
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] been very unhappy, mortified and indignant, and yet what could you
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] do? You could only perhaps ruin yourself, and, besides, Dounia
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] would not allow it; and fill up my letter with trifles when my heart
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was so full of sorrow, I could not. For a whole month the town was
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] full of gossip about this scandal, and it came to such a pass that
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia and I dared not even go to church on account of the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] contemptuous looks, whispers, and even remarks made aloud about
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] us. All our acquaintances avoided us, nobody even bowed to us in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] the street, and I learnt that some shopmen and clerks were intending
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to insult us in a shameful way, smearing the gates of our house with
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] pitch, so that the landlord began to tell us we must leave. All this
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was set going by Marfa Petrovna who managed to slander Dounia
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and throw dirt at her in every family. She knows everyone in the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] neighbourhood, and that month she was continually coming into the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] town, and as she is rather talkative and fond of gossiping about her
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] family affairs and particularly of complaining to all and each of her
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] husband—which is not at all right—so in a short time she had
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] spread her story not only in the town, but over the whole
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] surrounding district. It made me ill, but Dounia bore it better than I
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] did, and if only you could have seen how she endured it all and tried

[PAGE 42]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to comfort me and cheer me up! She is an angel! But by God’s
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] mercy, our sufferings were cut short: Mr. Svidrigaïlov returned to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] his senses and repented and, probably feeling sorry for Dounia, he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] laid before Marfa Petrovna a complete and unmistakable proof of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia’s innocence, in the form of a letter Dounia had been forced
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to write and give to him, before Marfa Petrovna came upon them in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] the garden. This letter, which remained in Mr. Svidrigaïlov’s hands
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] after her departure, she had written to refuse personal explanations
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and secret interviews, for which he was entreating her. In that letter
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] she reproached him with great heat and indignation for the baseness
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] of his behaviour in regard to Marfa Petrovna, reminding him that he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was the father and head of a family and telling him how infamous it
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was of him to torment and make unhappy a defenceless girl,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] unhappy enough already. Indeed, dear Rodya, the letter was so
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] nobly and touchingly written that I sobbed when I read it and to this
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] day I cannot read it without tears. Moreover, the evidence of the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] servants, too, cleared Dounia’s reputation; they had seen and known
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] a great deal more than Mr. Svidrigaïlov had himself supposed—as
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] indeed is always the case with servants. Marfa Petrovna was
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] completely taken aback, and ‘again crushed’ as she said herself to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] us, but she was completely convinced of Dounia’s innocence. The
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] very next day, being Sunday, she went straight to the Cathedral,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] knelt down and prayed with tears to Our Lady to give her strength
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to bear this new trial and to do her duty. Then she came straight
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] from the Cathedral to us, told us the whole story, wept bitterly and,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] fully penitent, she embraced Dounia and besought her to forgive her.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] The same morning without any delay, she went round to all the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] houses in the town and everywhere, shedding tears, she asserted in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] the most flattering terms Dounia’s innocence and the nobility of her
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] feelings and her behavior. What was more, she showed and read to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] everyone the letter in Dounia’s own handwriting to Mr. Svidrigaïlov
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and even allowed them to take copies of it—which I must say I think
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] was superfluous. In this way she was busy for several days in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] driving about the whole town, because some people had taken
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] offence through precedence having been given to others. And
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] therefore they had to take turns, so that in every house she was

[PAGE 43]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] expected before she arrived, and everyone knew that on such and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] such a day Marfa Petrovna would be reading the letter in such and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] such a place and people assembled for every reading of it, even
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] many who had heard it several times already both in their own
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] houses and in other people’s. In my opinion a great deal, a very
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] great deal of all this was unnecessary; but that’s Marfa Petrovna’s
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] character. Anyway she succeeded in completely re-establishing
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia’s reputation and the whole ignominy of this affair rested as
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] an indelible disgrace upon her husband, as the only person to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] blame, so that I really began to feel sorry for him; it was really
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] treating the crazy fellow too harshly. Dounia was at once asked to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] give lessons in several families, but she refused. All of a sudden
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] everyone began to treat her with marked respect and all this did
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] much to bring about the event by which, one may say, our whole
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] fortunes are now transformed. You must know, dear Rodya, that
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia has a suitor and that she has already consented to marry
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] him. I hasten to tell you all about the matter, and though it has been
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] arranged without asking your consent, I think you will not be
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] aggrieved with me or with your sister on that account, for you will
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] see that we could not wait and put off our decision till we heard
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] from you. And you could not have judged all the facts without being
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] on the spot. This was how it happened. He is already of the rank of a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] counsellor, Pyotr Petrovitch Luzhin, and is distantly related to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Marfa Petrovna, who has been very active in bringing the match
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] about. It began with his expressing through her his desire to make
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] our acquaintance. He was properly received, drank coffee with us
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and the very next day he sent us a letter in which he very
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] courteously made an offer and begged for a speedy and decided
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] answer. He is a very busy man and is in a great hurry to get to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Petersburg, so that every moment is precious to him. At first, of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] course, we were greatly surprised, as it had all happened so quickly
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and unexpectedly. We thought and talked it over the whole day. He is
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] a well-to-do man, to be depended upon, he has two posts in the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] government and has already made his fortune. It is true that he is
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] forty-five years old, but he is of a fairly prepossessing appearance
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and might still be thought attractive by women, and he is altogether

[PAGE 44]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] a very respectable and presentable man, only he seems a little
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] morose and somewhat conceited. But possibly that may only be the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] impression he makes at first sight. And beware, dear Rodya, when
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] he comes to Petersburg, as he shortly will do, beware of judging him
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] too hastily and severely, as your way is, if there is anything you do
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] not like in him at first sight. I give you this warning, although I feel
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] sure that he will make a favourable impression upon you. Moreover,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] in order to understand any man one must be deliberate and careful
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to avoid forming prejudices and mistaken ideas, which are very
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] difficult to correct and get over afterwards. And Pyotr Petrovitch,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] judging by many indications, is a thoroughly estimable man. At his
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] first visit, indeed, he told us that he was a practical man, but still he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] shares, as he expressed it, many of the convictions ‘of our most
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] rising generation’ and he is an opponent of all prejudices. He said a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] good deal more, for he seems a little conceited and likes to be
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] listened to, but this is scarcely a vice. I, of course, understood very
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] little of it, but Dounia explained to me that, though he is not a man
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] of great education, he is clever and seems to be good-natured. You
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] know your sister’s character, Rodya. She is a resolute, sensible,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] patient and generous girl, but she has a passionate heart, as I know
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] very well. Of course, there is no great love either on his side, or on
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] hers, but Dounia is a clever girl and has the heart of an angel, and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] will make it her duty to make her husband happy who on his side
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] will make her happiness his care. Of that we have no good reason to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] doubt, though it must be admitted the matter has been arranged in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] great haste. Besides he is a man of great prudence and he will see,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to be sure, of himself, that his own happiness will be the more
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] secure, the happier Dounia is with him. And as for some defects of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] character, for some habits and even certain differences of opinion—
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] which indeed are inevitable even in the happiest marriages—Dounia
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] has said that, as regards all that, she relies on herself, that there is
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] nothing to be uneasy about, and that she is ready to put up with a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] great deal, if only their future relationship can be an honourable
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and straightforward one. He struck me, for instance, at first, as
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] rather abrupt, but that may well come from his being an outspoken
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] man, and that is no doubt how it is. For instance, at his second visit,

[PAGE 45]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] after he had received Dounia’s consent, in the course of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] conversation, he declared that before making Dounia’s
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] acquaintance, he had made up his mind to marry a girl of good
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] reputation, without dowry and, above all, one who had experienced
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] poverty, because, as he explained, a man ought not to be indebted to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] his wife, but that it is better for a wife to look upon her husband as
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] her benefactor. I must add that he expressed it more nicely and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] politely than I have done, for I have forgotten his actual phrases and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] only remember the meaning. And, besides, it was obviously not said
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] of design, but slipped out in the heat of conversation, so that he tried
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] afterwards to correct himself and smooth it over, but all the same it
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] did strike me as somewhat rude, and I said so afterwards to Dounia.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] But Dounia was vexed, and answered that ‘words are not deeds,’
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and that, of course, is perfectly true. Dounia did not sleep all night
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] before she made up her mind, and, thinking that I was asleep, she
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] got out of bed and was walking up and down the room all night; at
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] last she knelt down before the ikon and prayed long and fervently
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and in the morning she told me that she had decided.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=122 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] “I have mentioned already that Pyotr Petrovitch is just setting
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] off for Petersburg, where he has a great deal of business, and he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] wants to open a legal bureau. He has been occupied for many years
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] in conducting civil and commercial litigation, and only the other
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] day he won an important case. He has to be in Petersburg because
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] he has an important case before the Senate. So, Rodya dear, he may
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] be of the greatest use to you, in every way indeed, and Dounia and I
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] have agreed that from this very day you could definitely enter upon
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] your career and might consider that your future is marked out and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] assured for you. Oh, if only this comes to pass! This would be such a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] benefit that we could only look upon it as a providential blessing.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia is dreaming of nothing else. We have even ventured already
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to drop a few words on the subject to Pyotr Petrovitch. He was
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] cautious in his answer, and said that, of course, as he could not get
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] on without a secretary, it would be better to be paying a salary to a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] relation than to a stranger, if only the former were fitted for the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] duties (as though there could be doubt of your being fitted!) but then
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] he expressed doubts whether your studies at the university would

[PAGE 46]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] leave you time for work at his office. The matter dropped for the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] time, but Dounia is thinking of nothing else now. She has been in a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] sort of fever for the last few days, and has already made a regular
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] plan for your becoming in the end an associate and even a partner
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] in Pyotr Petrovitch’s business, which might well be, seeing that you
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] are a student of law. I am in complete agreement with her, Rodya,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] and share all her plans and hopes, and think there is every
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] probability of realising them. And in spite of Pyotr Petrovitch’s
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] evasiveness, very natural at present (since he does not know you),
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Dounia is firmly persuaded that she will gain everything by her
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] good influence over her future husband; this she is reckoning upon.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] Of course we are careful not to talk of any of these more remote
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] plans to Pyotr Petrovitch, especially of your becoming his partner.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] He is a practical man and might take this very coldly, it might all
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] seem to him simply a day-dream. Nor has either Dounia or I
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] breathed a word to him of the great hopes we have of his helping us
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to pay for your university studies; we have not spoken of it in the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=1 f=f5] first place, because it will come to pass of itself, later on, and he will
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=1 f=f5] no doubt without wasting words offer to do it of himself, (as though
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=1 f=f5] he could refuse Dounia that) the more readily since you may by your
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=1 f=f5] own efforts become his right hand in the office, and receive this
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=1 f=f5] assistance not as a charity, but as a salary earned by your own
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=470 b=0 i=1 f=f5] work. Dounia wants to arrange it all like this and I quite agree with
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=1 f=f5] her. And we have not spoken of our plans for another reason, that is,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=1 f=f5] because I particularly wanted you to feel on an equal footing when
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=1 f=f5] you first meet him. When Dounia spoke to him with enthusiasm
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=1 f=f5] about you, he answered that one could never judge of a man without
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=1 f=f5] seeing him close, for oneself, and that he looked forward to forming
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=1 f=f5] his own opinion when he makes your acquaintance. Do you know,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=1 f=f5] my precious Rodya, I think that perhaps for some reasons (nothing
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=1 f=f5] to do with Pyotr Petrovitch though, simply for my own personal,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=1 f=f5] perhaps old-womanish, fancies) I should do better to go on living by
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=1 f=f5] myself, apart, than with them, after the wedding. I am convinced
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=668 b=0 i=1 f=f5] that he will be generous and delicate enough to invite me and to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=686 b=0 i=1 f=f5] urge me to remain with my daughter for the future, and if he has
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=704 b=0 i=1 f=f5] said nothing about it hitherto, it is simply because it has been taken

[PAGE 47]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=74 b=0 i=1 f=f5] for granted; but I shall refuse. I have noticed more than once in my
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=92 b=0 i=1 f=f5] life that husbands don’t quite get on with their mothers-in-law, and I
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=1 f=f5] don’t want to be the least bit in anyone’s way, and for my own sake,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=128 b=0 i=1 f=f5] too, would rather be quite independent, so long as I have a crust of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=146 b=0 i=1 f=f5] bread of my own, and such children as you and Dounia. If possible,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=164 b=0 i=1 f=f5] I would settle somewhere near you, for the most joyful piece of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=1 f=f5] news, dear Rodya, I have kept for the end of my letter: know then,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=200 b=0 i=1 f=f5] my dear boy, that we may, perhaps, be all together in a very short
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=218 b=0 i=1 f=f5] time and may embrace one another again after a separation of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=1 f=f5] almost three years! It is settled for certain that Dounia and I are to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=254 b=0 i=1 f=f5] set off for Petersburg, exactly when I don’t know, but very, very
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=1 f=f5] soon, possibly in a week. It all depends on Pyotr Petrovitch who will
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=290 b=0 i=1 f=f5] let us know when he has had time to look round him in Petersburg.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=1 f=f5] To suit his own arrangements he is anxious to have the ceremony as
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=1 f=f5] soon as possible, even before the fast of Our Lady, if it could be
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=1 f=f5] managed, or if that is too soon to be ready, immediately after. Oh,

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=380 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Perhaps he had daughters growing up like that, “looking like ladies and

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=0 f=f3] In spite of those strange words he felt very wretched. He sat down on

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=506 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Poor girl!” he said, looking at the empty corner where she had sat

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=74 b=0 i=0 f=f3] way… to the devil, I suppose, so that the rest may remain chaste, and not be

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=182 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “But where am I going?” he thought suddenly. “Strange, I came out for

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=0 f=f3] With Razumihin he had got on, or, at least, he was more unreserved and

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=344 b=0 i=0 f=f3] on horseback; he watched them with curious eyes and forgot about them
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=362 b=0 i=0 f=f3] before they had vanished from his sight. Once he stood still and counted his
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=380 b=0 i=0 f=f3] money; he found he had thirty copecks. “Twenty to the policeman, three to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=398 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Nastasya for the letter, so I must have given forty-seven or fifty to the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=416 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Marmeladovs yesterday,” he thought, reckoning it up for some unknown
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=434 b=0 i=0 f=f3] reason, but he soon forgot with what object he had taken the money out of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=452 b=0 i=0 f=f3] his pocket. He recalled it on passing an eating-house or tavern, and felt that
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=470 b=0 i=0 f=f3] he was hungry…. Going into the tavern he drank a glass of vodka and ate a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=488 b=0 i=0 f=f3] pie of some sort. He finished eating it as he walked away. It was a long
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=506 b=0 i=0 f=f3] while since he had taken vodka and it had an effect upon him at once,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=524 b=0 i=0 f=f3] though he only drank a wineglassful. His legs felt suddenly heavy and a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=542 b=0 i=0 f=f3] great drowsiness came upon him. He turned homewards, but reaching
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=560 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Petrovsky Ostrov he stopped completely exhausted, turned off the road into
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=578 b=0 i=0 f=f3] the bushes, sank down upon the grass and instantly fell asleep.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=0 f=f3] In a morbid condition of the brain, dreams often have a singular
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=614 b=0 i=0 f=f3] actuality, vividness, and extraordinary semblance of reality. At times
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=632 b=0 i=0 f=f3] monstrous images are created, but the setting and the whole picture are so
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=650 b=0 i=0 f=f3] truth-like and filled with details so delicate, so unexpectedly, but so
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=668 b=0 i=0 f=f3] artistically consistent, that the dreamer, were he an artist like Pushkin or
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=686 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Turgenev even, could never have invented them in the waking state. Such

[PAGE 64]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=74 b=0 i=0 f=f3] sick dreams always remain long in the memory and make a powerful
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=92 b=0 i=0 f=f3] impression on the overwrought and deranged nervous system.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=110 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Raskolnikov had a fearful dream. He dreamt he was back in his
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=128 b=0 i=0 f=f3] childhood in the little town of his birth. He was a child about seven years
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=146 b=0 i=0 f=f3] old, walking into the country with his father on the evening of a holiday. It
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=164 b=0 i=0 f=f3] was a grey and heavy day, the country was exactly as he remembered it;
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=182 b=0 i=0 f=f3] indeed he recalled it far more vividly in his dream than he had done in
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=200 b=0 i=0 f=f3] memory. The little town stood on a level flat as bare as the hand, not even a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=218 b=0 i=0 f=f3] willow near it; only in the far distance, a copse lay, a dark blur on the very
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=236 b=0 i=0 f=f3] edge of the horizon. A few paces beyond the last market garden stood a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=254 b=0 i=0 f=f3] tavern, a big tavern, which had always aroused in him a feeling of aversion,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=272 b=0 i=0 f=f3] even of fear, when he walked by it with his father. There was always a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=290 b=0 i=0 f=f3] crowd there, always shouting, laughter and abuse, hideous hoarse singing
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=308 b=0 i=0 f=f3] and often fighting. Drunken and horrible-looking figures were hanging
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=326 b=0 i=0 f=f3] about the tavern. He used to cling close to his father, trembling all over
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=344 b=0 i=0 f=f3] when he met them. Near the tavern the road became a dusty track, the dust
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=362 b=0 i=0 f=f3] of which was always black. It was a winding road, and about a hundred
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=380 b=0 i=0 f=f3] paces further on, it turned to the right to the graveyard. In the middle of the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=398 b=0 i=0 f=f3] graveyard stood a stone church with a green cupola where he used to go to
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=416 b=0 i=0 f=f3] mass two or three times a year with his father and mother, when a service
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=434 b=0 i=0 f=f3] was held in memory of his grandmother, who had long been dead, and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=452 b=0 i=0 f=f3] whom he had never seen. On these occasions they used to take on a white
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=470 b=0 i=0 f=f3] dish tied up in a table napkin a special sort of rice pudding with raisins
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=488 b=0 i=0 f=f3] stuck in it in the shape of a cross. He loved that church, the old-fashioned,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=506 b=0 i=0 f=f3] unadorned ikons and the old priest with the shaking head. Near his
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=524 b=0 i=0 f=f3] grandmother’s grave, which was marked by a stone, was the little grave of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=542 b=0 i=0 f=f3] his younger brother who had died at six months old. He did not remember
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=560 b=0 i=0 f=f3] him at all, but he had been told about his little brother, and whenever he
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=578 b=0 i=0 f=f3] visited the graveyard he used religiously and reverently to cross himself and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=596 b=0 i=0 f=f3] to bow down and kiss the little grave. And now he dreamt that he was
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=614 b=0 i=0 f=f3] walking with his father past the tavern on the way to the graveyard; he was
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=632 b=0 i=0 f=f3] holding his father’s hand and looking with dread at the tavern. A peculiar
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=650 b=0 i=0 f=f3] circumstance attracted his attention: there seemed to be some kind of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=668 b=0 i=0 f=f3] festivity going on, there were crowds of gaily dressed townspeople, peasant
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=686 b=0 i=0 f=f3] women, their husbands, and riff-raff of all sorts, all singing and all more or
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=704 b=0 i=0 f=f3] less drunk. Near the entrance of the tavern stood a cart, but a strange cart. It

[PAGE 65]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=74 b=0 i=0 f=f3] was one of those big carts usually drawn by heavy cart-horses and laden
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=92 b=0 i=0 f=f3] with casks of wine or other heavy goods. He always liked looking at those
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=110 b=0 i=0 f=f3] great cart-horses, with their long manes, thick legs, and slow even pace,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=128 b=0 i=0 f=f3] drawing along a perfect mountain with no appearance of effort, as though it
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=146 b=0 i=0 f=f3] were easier going with a load than without it. But now, strange to say, in the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=164 b=0 i=0 f=f3] shafts of such a cart he saw a thin little sorrel beast, one of those peasants’
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=182 b=0 i=0 f=f3] nags which he had often seen straining their utmost under a heavy load of
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=200 b=0 i=0 f=f3] wood or hay, especially when the wheels were stuck in the mud or in a rut.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=218 b=0 i=0 f=f3] And the peasants would beat them so cruelly, sometimes even about the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=236 b=0 i=0 f=f3] nose and eyes, and he felt so sorry, so sorry for them that he almost cried,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=254 b=0 i=0 f=f3] and his mother always used to take him away from the window. All of a
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=272 b=0 i=0 f=f3] sudden there was a great uproar of shouting, singing and the balalaïka, and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=290 b=0 i=0 f=f3] from the tavern a number of big and very drunken peasants came out,
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=308 b=0 i=0 f=f3] wearing red and blue shirts and coats thrown over their shoulders.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=326 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Get in, get in!” shouted one of them, a young thick-necked peasant
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=344 b=0 i=0 f=f3] with a fleshy face red as a carrot. “I’ll take you all, get in!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=362 b=0 i=0 f=f3] But at once there was an outbreak of laughter and exclamations in the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=380 b=0 i=0 f=f3] crowd.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=398 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Take us all with a beast like that!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=416 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Why, Mikolka, are you crazy to put a nag like that in such a cart?”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “And this mare is twenty if she is a day, mates!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Get in, I’ll take you all,” Mikolka shouted again, leaping first into the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=470 b=0 i=0 f=f3] cart, seizing the reins and standing straight up in front. “The bay has gone
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=488 b=0 i=0 f=f3] with Matvey,” he shouted from the cart—“and this brute, mates, is just
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=506 b=0 i=0 f=f3] breaking my heart, I feel as if I could kill her. She’s just eating her head off.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=524 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Get in, I tell you! I’ll make her gallop! She’ll gallop!” and he picked up the
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=542 b=0 i=0 f=f3] whip, preparing himself with relish to flog the little mare.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=560 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Get in! Come along!” The crowd laughed. “D’you hear, she’ll gallop!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Gallop indeed! She has not had a gallop in her for the last ten years!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=596 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “She’ll jog along!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=614 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Don’t you mind her, mates, bring a whip each of you, get ready!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=632 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “All right! Give it to her!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=650 b=0 i=0 f=f3] They all clambered into Mikolka’s cart, laughing and making jokes. Six
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=668 b=0 i=0 f=f3] men got in and there was still room for more. They hauled in a fat, rosy-
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=686 b=0 i=0 f=f3] cheeked woman. She was dressed in red cotton, in a pointed, beaded
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=704 b=0 i=0 f=f3] headdress and thick leather shoes; she was cracking nuts and laughing. The

[PAGE 66]
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=74 b=0 i=0 f=f3] crowd round them was laughing too and indeed, how could they help
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=92 b=0 i=0 f=f3] laughing? That wretched nag was to drag all the cartload of them at a

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=128 b=0 i=0 f=f3] Mikolka. With the cry of “now,” the mare tugged with all her might, but far

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=164 b=0 i=0 f=f3] gasping and shrinking from the blows of the three whips which were
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=182 b=0 i=0 f=f3] showered upon her like hail. The laughter in the cart and in the crowd was

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=218 b=0 i=0 f=f3] though he supposed she really could gallop.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=236 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Let me get in, too, mates,” shouted a young man in the crowd whose
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=254 b=0 i=0 f=f3] appetite was aroused.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=272 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Get in, all get in,” cried Mikolka, “she will draw you all. I’ll beat her to

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=308 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Father, father,” he cried, “father, what are they doing? Father, they are
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=326 b=0 i=0 f=f3] beating the poor horse!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=344 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Come along, come along!” said his father. “They are drunken and
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=77 y=362 b=0 i=0 f=f3] foolish, they are in fun; come away, don’t look!” and he tried to draw him

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=434 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Beat her to death,” cried Mikolka, “it’s come to that. I’ll do for her!”
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=452 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “What are you about, are you a Christian, you devil?” shouted an old

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=488 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Did anyone ever see the like? A wretched nag like that pulling such a

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=524 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “You’ll kill her,” shouted the third.
[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=542 b=0 i=0 f=f3] “Don’t meddle! It’s my property, I’ll do what I choose. Get in, more of

[L s=15.0 r=1.00 x=99 y=578 b=0 i=0 f=f3] All at once laughter broke into a roar and covered everything: the mare,
